t_ch: 看来我们得留下来处理些大人的事务了。
t_en: So we're... staying behind to take care of the grownup stuff?

t_ch: 别心急，瓦尔特，会有你活跃的机会的。
t_en: Chin up, Welt. You'll get your chance out in the field.

t_ch: 三月七也需要有人照顾，我来搭把手吧。
t_en: Someone should be taking care of March 7th, too. I can do that.

t_ch: 我也没有异议，悉听各位的安排。
t_en: I'm fine with any arrangement you have for me.

t_ch: 看来是达成共识了，那你们两人跟我来观景车厢吧。
t_en: Great, sounds like we're all good! Come with me to the Parlor Car then, you two.

t_ch: 以防万一，我在出发前准备了一个「后备方案」，没想到这么快就到了揭秘的时候。
t_en: I prepared a backup plan before our departure, but I didn't count on having to reveal it so soon.

t_ch: 地面小分队的成员到齐了，让列车长宣布「后备方案」吧。
t_en: Now that our ground exploration team is assembled, I'll let the conductor reveal our backup plan.

t_ch: 嗯，此行凶险，列车长和领航员为你们准备了一份特别的「开拓」礼物——
t_en: Yes. Seeing how this is going to be a perilous expedition, Pom-Pom and the Navigator have come together to prepare a special trailblazing gift for you!

t_ch: 一、节、车、厢帕！
t_en: Your~ very~ own~ car~!

t_ch: 什么玩意？
t_en: Our what now?

t_ch: 星穹列车要分家了？
t_en: The Express is splitting up?

t_ch: 可以给我派对车厢吗？
t_en: Can I have the Party Car instead?

t_ch: 翁法罗斯不在星际和平通信的服务范围内，缺乏远程联络的手段。
t_en: Amphoreus is outside the Interastral Peace Communications' coverage zone, so there's no chance of remote communication when you're there.

t_ch: 为了给你们最大限度的支援，我们计划将星穹列车的一节车厢分离出去，当作降落舱使用，落地后也能充当安全屋。
t_en: Uncoupling and using one of the Express's cars as your landing capsule is the best way we can ease your exploration. It can also be your safe house once you're on Amphoreus.

t_ch: 放心，车厢上有独立的推进器，一定能把你们送进翁法罗斯，找个安全的地点着陆。
t_en: The car you're getting has its own thrusters, so just buckle up, and It'll take you into Amphoreus and land you somewhere safe.

t_ch: 这列车是否有些太好用了？
t_en: The Express is more multipurpose than I thought...

t_ch: 这是紧急情况下的备案，可不要觉得列车能随便当积木拆哦！
t_en: Only in extenuating circumstances~! Don't go thinking you can detach cars from the Express every which way, alright!

t_ch: 这下力大砖飞了，冲啊！
t_en: Woohoo, a powerful engine to send cars blasting off in space!

t_ch: 不可以！这是紧急情况下的备案，可不要觉得列车能随便当积木拆！
t_en: Oh, no you don't! Only in extenuating circumstances! Don't go thinking you can detach cars from the Express every which way, alright!

t_ch: 我会看好{F#她}{M#他}的。
t_en: I'll make sure to keep an eye on {M#him.}{F#her.}

t_ch: 准备好了就出发吧，期待各位的好消息帕。
t_en: Get going whenever you're ready. Pom-Pom can't wait to hear about your exciting discoveries!

t_ch: 降落用的车厢已经准备好了。两位「开拓」小先锋，准备好面对真正的未知了吗？
t_en: The landing car is ready. My dear trailblazing vanguards, are you all set to dive into the unknown?

t_ch: 准备好了。
t_en: I'm ready.

t_ch: 向着「永恒之地」——出发吧！
t_en: Go ahead and journey into The Eternal Land!

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: Hold on a moment.

t_ch: 还要做点心理建设？别太久哦。
t_en: Oh? Need to psyche yourself up? Don't keep us waiting too long.

t_ch: （三月七，没事吧？）
t_en: (Is March 7th okay?)

t_ch: 几束薄冰将昏迷的三月七锁在床上，原本温馨的房间如今透着一股凄冷。
t_en: A number of thin sheets of ice have shackled the unconscious March 7th to her bed. The once warm bedroom is now permeated with a bitter cold.

t_ch: 冰中的少女恬静无瑕…一如她曾在太空中遗世漂泊的模样。
t_en: The young girl lays silent and flawless in the ice... Just as she did back when she was drifting through space.

t_ch: 少女蜷在剔透的冰层中，紧阖双眼、一语不发。
t_en: Curled up in the clear ice, the young girl remains silent with her eyes fixed shut.

t_ch: 虽然这些冰层并不冻手，但平时活泼雀跃的少女陷入静默，却格外叫人心生悲凉。
t_en: Although the ice is not cold to the touch, there is something particularly saddening about seeing a young girl who is normally so full of life fall into complete silence.

t_ch: 瓦尔特•杨与星期日似乎在讨论三月七的症状、筹划下一步安排，还是不要打扰他们了。
t_en: Welt Yang and Sunday seem to be discussing March 7th's symptoms and planning their next steps. It's best not to bother them.

t_ch: 翁法罗斯之外，银河中某个熟悉的角落……
t_en: Outside Amphoreus, in a familiar corner of the Cosmos...

t_ch: 真是一段■■■的冒险啊——请在■■■处插入任意你觉得合适的形容词！
t_en: Ah, an utterly ■■■ adventure! Feel free to replace ■■■ with whatever description you see fit!

t_ch: 你肯定也跟我一样好奇吧：那么，接下来会发生什么？
t_en: I bet you're as curious as I am. So! What's going to happen next?

t_ch: 别着急，因为…就算着急也没用嘛。不如先跟我来，让我给你讲讲发生在银河另一端的故事……
t_en: Heh, keep it together. All that excitement isn't going to get you anywhere, you know. Why don't you follow me into another tale on the other side of the cosmos...?

t_ch: 看呐，这里是哪？是黑塔空间站——天才的营地，科学的摇篮。
t_en: Ta-da! Recognize this place? Herta Space Station — the base of a genius and the cradle of science.

t_ch: 瞧瞧这些黑塔人偶，做工多么精致？我可不止一次听说，有些人在背后暗暗地夸赞：「她真好看」……
t_en: Look at these puppets! Aren't they dearly exquisite? Let's just say I've already heard many others secretly gushing, "She looks so pretty" on more than one occasion...

t_ch: 不过要我说，她们对于黑塔女士美貌的还原，连十分之一…啊不，连百分之一都谈不上。这可不是恭维，我只是在陈述客观事实……
t_en: If you ask me though, they barely did justice to Madam Herta's beauty! Maybe just <unbreak>10%</unbreak>... no, not even 1% of the real deal! Now I'm no fawner here, just stating an objective fact...

t_ch: 还记得这一幕吗？多么让人精神振奋的画面！尊贵的天才俱乐部#83，黑塔女士，正在向伟大的博识尊发问，想要从祂身上求得「神性」的奥秘！
t_en: Remember this scene? Gah, it evoked so much emotion in me! The esteemed Madam Herta, Genius Society #83, asking the great Nous a question! An attempt to gain a peek into the divinities' mysteries through THEM!

t_ch: 书接上回，足以改写银河历史的天才传奇就从这里开……
t_en: Alright, so continuing from where we left off last time, this is where everything would begin for the genius smart enough to rewrite the history of the entire cos—

t_ch: …我说，第四面镜——你在跟谁说话？
t_en: ...Tsk, Fourth Mirror. Who exactly are you talking to?

t_ch: 呃…我这不是想给咱们的出场增加点仪式感嘛，黑塔女士。
t_en: Wh—Why, I was just ramping it up for our grand appearance, Madam Herta.

t_ch: 噢，初登场的仪式感……
t_en: Hm. So, a dramatic debut...?

t_ch: …有那种东西才怪吧？！我要是真跟机器头说上话了倒还好，但现在的问题是…祂根本没有反应！
t_en: ...But what are the odds of that happening!? Sure, slightly plausible if I did manage to talk to Droidhead, but guess what's the problem...? THEY have no reaction whatsoever!

t_ch: 呃…可是黑塔女士，您看这里…只从这画面上看，您明明都已经站在博识尊面前了啊？
t_en: Err... But look at this, Madam Herta... You're standing before Nous here! Right there.

t_ch: 哦，那个啊？只是个全息投影而已，又不可能真把机器头本尊召唤到这里。
t_en: Oh, that? That's a hologram. What? I can't possibly summon the real Droidhead here, can I?

t_ch: …原来你只是在对着个投影念台词啊？！
t_en: ...You mean to say you've just been reciting your lines before a hologram!?

t_ch: 这叫仪式感，你不是最看重这个了吗？
t_en: Yeah, creating dramatic value. Isn't that what you're most obsessed with?

t_ch: 不说这些没用的了。你可知道，要给博识尊发送一段信息是多不容易的事，我可是下了血本——
t_en: Moving on. Don't try, but you wouldn't even begin to imagine how difficult it is to send a message to Nous. And I went all out for it...

t_ch: ——为了给「谒见系统」供能，我必须调用整个空间站的能源，让它进入静默，事后再重启。这一来一回，你知道背后的成本有多高吗？
t_en: Powering the Audience System alone required me to reconfigure the energy supply of the entire space station! I had to switch it to Hibernate mode before rebooting everything. Do you have any idea how much that costs?

t_ch: …有多高？
t_en: ...How much? How much?
Uh...

t_ch: …钱的事不归我管。你不如去问艾丝妲。
t_en: ...I'm not in charge of finance. Go pose that to Asta instead.

t_ch: 原来你也不知道啊！
t_en: So you don't know either!

t_ch: 我可是黑塔本塔，「智识」的令使，货真价实的天才——控制成本？这种事太庸俗。
t_en: I am Herta herself, the Emanator of Erudition and a true-blue genius. Why would I be holding the purse strings? That menial task is beneath me.

t_ch: 说得太对了，黑塔女士！对于您这样的天才，公司就该无条件地提供一切资源，放任您在自己感兴趣的课题上肆意挥霍！
t_en: You're absolutely right, Madam Herta! The IPC should really be providing a genius like you with all that you require, so you can freely dive into any research that tickles your fancy!

t_ch: 既然如此，黑塔本塔，「智识」的令使，货真价实的天才…您现在打算怎么办？
t_en: With that said, Herta herself, Emanator of Erudition, and true-blue genius... What do you plan to do now?

t_ch: 还能怎么办？当然是查出「谒见系统」的故障，然后把它给解决了。
t_en: What else can I do? Identify what's malfunctioning within the Audience System and fix it, of course.

t_ch: 呃…恕我直言，黑塔女士，您擅长的领域的确很多。但您确定「动手能力」还是自己的强项之一吗？
t_en: Um... Don't take offense to my candid words, Madam Herta. You may be proficient in many aspects, but... is hands-on work really one of your strong suits?

t_ch: 换作是我，我会建议您联系斯蒂芬……
t_en: Um... I humbly suggest for you to contact Stephen instead...

t_ch: 联系他，和他讲我砸重金只为往机器头脑袋里塞一句话，结果把一切都搞砸了？
t_en: Contact him and expose how I shelled out a bomb to send a message to Droidhead, only to screw up everything else?

t_ch: 好好好，我明白了……
t_en: Mm, alright, alright. I get it...

t_ch: 明白就好。
t_en: Good that you do.

t_ch: 看吧，那就是「谒见系统」的终端。走，跟我一起去检查一下。
t_en: There. That's the Audience System's terminal. Come, walk with me. We'll check what's wrong with it.

t_ch: 遵命，女士！噢，可惜我不会走，只会飘……
t_en: Aye-aye, ma'am! Oh, but I can't walk. I can only float...

t_ch: 你今天的俏皮话好像格外多啊？
t_en: What's with your witty remarks today?

t_ch: 唔…这就是「谒见系统」？看起来跟模拟宇宙没什么区别嘛。
t_en: Hmm... This is the Audience System? It looks just like the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: 你说的没错。二者用的是同一套底层逻辑，本质上是一回事。
t_en: Keen observation. They share the same fundamental logic, and are pretty much identical in that regard.

t_ch: 噢，懂了。这就叫「有效借鉴」吧？嘿嘿……
t_en: Ahh, I see. We call this... referencing, and not appropriation, right? Heh...

t_ch: 你要是再贫嘴，我保证，我助手的名字就要变成「第五面镜」了。
t_en: One more stupid remark from you and you can watch Fifth Mirror reference your role, because I'm going to be dropping you as my assistant.

t_ch: 说正事，「谒见系统」被我划分成了三个扇区：「能源区」、「算力区」、还有「通信区」。每个扇区各自独立，只有系统启动时才会通过「智识」的命途串联起来。
t_en: Focus now. The Audience System is divided into three zones: Energy Supply, Computing, and Communications. Each zone operates independently, and will only be integrated via the Path of Erudition when the system is booted.

t_ch: 想跟机器头说上一句话可不容易。如此缜密的系统，平时想要测试或是定期维护也是难上加难，出点小问题也正常。
t_en: Sending a message to Droidhead involves more intricacies than you can imagine. If routine trials and maintenance on such an elaborately sophisticated system are already as bad as squaring circles, then it's no surprise for glitches like this to crop up.

t_ch: 原来如此。那黑塔女士，经过你的专业判断——是哪个扇区出故障了？
t_en: I see. So, Madam Herta, can you use your professional judgment to tell which zone the glitch is in?

t_ch: 简单，当然是三个全都出问题了。
t_en: Of course. All three.

t_ch: 没什么大不了的，只需要一点小小的技术性调整。
t_en: No sweat. They'll be up and running again after some simple technical tweaking.

t_ch: 三个扇区是独立分开的，所以排查问题也得一个一个来。你要是准备好了，我们就先去「能源区」检查下情况。
t_en: The three zones are compartmentalized, so we're going to have to troubleshoot them one at a time. Let's start with the Energy Supply Zone. Get ready, and then we'll go over.

t_ch: 我可是面镜子，黑塔女士！只要镜子里的您准备好了，那我就准备好了。
t_en: I'm just a mirror, Madam Herta! If you're ready, then your reflection in me will also be ready to go.

t_ch: 咯咯咯…这「能源区」…怎么这么冷啊？咯咯咯…我的镜面，都快要结霜了！
t_en: Brr... Why is this Energy Supply Zone... so cold? Brr... I—I'm already frosting up!

t_ch: 你以为呢？整个黑塔空间站的能源都被我转化成了虚数态，蓄存在这个扇区。要是不配置点超低温环境，空间站早就被炸上天了。
t_en: Hmph, why else? I've converted the energy core that supplies all of Herta Space Station into Imaginary state and stored it in this zone. If not for the ultra-low temperature here, the space station would have long blasted off into the sky.

t_ch: 咯咯咯…但是…我们本来不就…在天上吗？
t_en: Brrr... But... aren't we already... in the sky?

t_ch: …作为一面镜子，你的幽默感是不是有点过剩了？
t_en: ...You're just a mirror. I don't need you to take on comic relief too.

t_ch: 那些家伙是哪来的？坏死数据？活性化的系统锈斑？不应该啊…总不能是军团又打过来了吧？
t_en: Where did those things come from? Corrupted data? Reanimated systemic rust? No, not likely... It can't be the Legion's second ambush, right...?

t_ch: 不管了，反正都得清理干净。跟紧我，可别轻易碎了！
t_en: Meh, whatever. We have to get rid of them anyway. Alright, stay close and try to avoid shattering to pieces!

t_ch: 女士，这是什么？
t_en: What's this, ma'am?

t_ch: 那个啊，应该就是个普通的忆泡吧？没什么特别的，其它舱段里也到处都是。
t_en: Mm, looks like one of those Memory Bubbles. It's not worth that much attention. You'll find many more in the other zones.

t_ch: 嗯…有点意思。
t_en: Hmm... is that so.

t_ch: 这…这是什么？！哪来这么多恶心虫子？
t_en: Wh—What in the...!? How did all these disgusting bugs get in here?

t_ch: 要想见到机器头，算力至少得匹配上。每秒执行的浮点运算达不到个几万垓次，祂压根就感受不到你的存在。
t_en: We're going to need out-of-this-world computing capability if we hope to meet Droidhead. If we can't churn out calculations faster than a yottaFLOPS computer, then we can forget about having THEM notice our existence.

t_ch: 但算力太高也有坏处，就是会引来这群「算力蜇虫」——「繁育」子嗣中的一支，也不知道是怎么进化出来的。
t_en: But computers with too high a computing capability tend to attract Computing Stings like them. No one knows how these creatures evolved from their progenitor — the Propagation.

t_ch: 生命，很神奇吧？不过还是比不上一面会说话的镜子，哈。
t_en: Ah, the mysteries of life. Hah! But even so, they can't compare to a talking mirror~

t_ch: 顺带一提，黑塔女士——你既然有算力如此强大的超级计算机，那它平时到底在…呃…算些什么？
t_en: A question on the side, Madam Herta: You have these powerful supercomputers and all, so... um, what are they normally programmed to calculate?

t_ch: 这重要吗？我只需要让它运转起来，能引起机器头的注意就行了。
t_en: Is that important? All that matters is that they work and are able to attract Droidhead's attention.

t_ch: 唉…有空的话，您还是跟艾丝妲小姐聊聊吧。
t_en: *sigh* ...I suggest meeting Miss Asta for a chat when you have time.

t_ch: 这里也有个忆泡……
t_en: There's a Memory Bubble here too...

t_ch: 那么关心它干嘛？
t_en: Why are you so interested in it?

t_ch: 好吧，我就是觉得有点…诡异？
t_en: Okay, I'll admit it. I find them somewhat... creepy?

t_ch: 救命！虫子的体液，崩到我的镜面上了！
t_en: Ew, yuck! Help me! Goo from those bugs got onto my reflective surface!

t_ch: 自己的事情自己做，用手擦掉不就行了？
t_en: Clean up after yourself. Can't you just wipe it off with your hands?

t_ch: 哈、哈！您真幽默……
t_en: ...Ahaha! Good one there...

t_ch: 解决了烦人的虫子，「算力区」也算打扫干净了。
t_en: With those annoying bugs squashed, we're done fixing the Computing Zone too.

t_ch: 计算机很快就能恢复运转，距离谒见机器头就只差最后一步。
t_en: These computers will get up to speed soon. Now we're just one final step away from requesting an audience with Droidhead.

t_ch: 最后一个扇区…是叫「通信区」来着？
t_en: Our last stop is the... Communications Zone?

t_ch: 你记得还挺清楚嘛。走吧，去看看那边出了什么状况。
t_en: Oh? You have a good memory. Let's go see what's wrong in that zone.

t_ch: 这…这是？黑塔女士，没想到连这里都有你的人偶。
t_en: Wh—What's all this? I didn't think your puppets would be in here too, Madam Herta.

t_ch: 「通信区」，顾名思义，就是负责建立空间站与机器头之间沟通桥梁的扇区。
t_en: Unsurprisingly, the Communications Zone is where a bridge for communication will be formed between Droidhead and the space station.

t_ch: 将「智识」的命途能量集中于一处，然后向星神发射一条「纽带」——等于从背后拍了拍某人的肩膀，他的反应肯定是回头看你一眼。
t_en: Aggregating the Path Energy of the Erudition, then firing a "bond" at the Aeon. This is as good as tapping someone on the shoulder, who's sure to turn back to look at you.

t_ch: 但这项工作可不简单，且不说前置准备有多麻烦，机器头的一瞥…稍有不慎就可能毁掉整个空间站。
t_en: Alas, our work is never this simple. Putting aside all that onerous legwork, a single glance from Droidhead... could vaporize the entire space station if we're ill-prepared.

t_ch: 噢…所以让空间站进入静默是一种保护手段，避免让设备运转过载甚至报废？
t_en: I see... So letting the space station go into hibernation is a protective measure of sorts? To prevent it from slipping into operational overload and turning into a scrap world?

t_ch: 悟性挺高的嘛，镜子。
t_en: Hm, very perceptive for a mirror.

t_ch: 耳濡目染，耳濡目染——虽然我既没耳朵也没眼睛，哈！
t_en: Mirror see (and hear), mirror do! Though I neither have eyes nor ears, haha!

t_ch: 这片扇区…好像还挺正常的？这些人偶也都在运作，没看出有什么故障……
t_en: This zone... seems to be functioning fine? These puppets are doing their job, and I don't see any obvious malfunction...

t_ch: 这可不好说啊。
t_en: Things may look fine only on the surface.

t_ch: 黑塔女士，您身后——
t_en: Madam Herta, behind you—

t_ch: …嗯？她想干嘛？
t_en: ...Hm? What does she want?

t_ch: 让我看看，这个人偶…应该是#2757。
t_en: Let me take a look. This puppet... should be #2757.

t_ch: 这、这你是怎么认出来的？！
t_en: H—How did you tell!?

t_ch: 嘘！别打岔，我在检查人偶的状态。#2757……
t_en: Shh! Don't distract me when I'm checking the puppet's parameters. #2757...

t_ch: 嗯…奇怪了……
t_en: Hmm... That's strange...

t_ch: 呃…怎么了，黑塔女士？
t_en: Er... What's wrong, Madam Herta?

t_ch: 「人格映射模块」…已关闭。奇怪，这项参数应该是默认打开的才对吧？
t_en: The "Replica Personality Module"... is off. That can't be. This parameter is set to be enabled by default.

t_ch: 「蛮不讲理模块」…也关闭了？这个更不合理，我前几天才检查过一遍，确保所有人偶都开着它……
t_en: And the "Utterly Absurd Module"... has also been disabled? Something's definitely wrong here. I checked a couple days ago to make sure every puppet has it switched on...

t_ch: 「无差别攻击模块」……
t_en: The "Attack Anything Everything Module"...

t_ch: 不是，为什么还有这种预设啊？！
t_en: No, wait. Why do you even have such presets!?

t_ch: …已打开。
t_en: ...is enabled.

t_ch: 发现。入侵。
t_en: Intruder. Detected.

t_ch: 这下坏了——镜子，帮我！
t_en: Drats. Come and help me, mirror...

t_ch: 无差别。攻击！
t_en: Anything, everything. Attack!

t_ch: 哎呀，可真是千钧一发…所以说，为什么这里会出现「记忆」的迷因？
t_en: Egads, what a close shave... But why are these Remembrance memes even showing up here?

t_ch: 可不止这一个。看那边——#7631，#10136…都是一样的问题。
t_en: That's not the only one. Look at them. #7631 and #10136... are affected too.

t_ch: 就不能远程把它们的参数调回来吗？
t_en: Can't you do a factory reset on them?

t_ch: 很遗憾，不能。因为「拒绝配合模块」已经被勾选上了——连我也绕不过它的权限，嘻嘻。
t_en: Sorry to disappoint. The "Absolutely Not Module" has been enabled, so not even I get to bypass its permissions. Whoopsie.

t_ch: …您到底是出于什么心理设计的这些玩意？
t_en: ...What were you really trying to achieve with these creations?

t_ch: 当时就是设计着玩而已。
t_en: Just some toys to amuse myself with.

t_ch: 寻找污染源头的事可以晚点再说。既然我本人已经到场，也就不需要这些人偶了。来吧，把残次品统统送进维修厂！
t_en: Finding the source of the corruption can wait. These puppets no longer have use now that I'm here. Take them all out! Defective goods ought to be sent back to the repair center!

t_ch: 为了空间站考虑，我建议您下手别太重……
t_en: I... urge you to go easy on them, for the sake of the space station...

t_ch: 这么深入的地方，居然也有忆泡？
t_en: A Memory Bubble tucked away this far in?

t_ch: 嘻…我有些头绪了，有趣。
t_en: Heh... I think I know what this is about now. This is interesting.

t_ch: 谜底可以留到晚点再揭晓…先解决有故障的人偶吧？
t_en: But we can leave that for later... Let's take care of the malfunctioning puppets first.

t_ch: 啊，#0988——我的得意之作！
t_en: Ah, #0988. The pride of my creations!

t_ch: …我都不知道该不该问了。
t_en: ...Should I even be curious...?

t_ch: 在它之前的人偶全都一模一样，千篇一律，无聊得很。我记得很清楚，从#0988开始，我会随机往人偶里塞一些小惊喜……
t_en: Puppets before her were all as dull as brick walls, mundane and uninspired. I distinctly recall #0988 being the first puppet I imbued with... a magical surprise. Some other puppets that came later also got to enjoy similar privileges.

t_ch: 虽然但是，谁问您了？
t_en: ...Good to know, but did anyone ask!?

t_ch: 别怕，镜子——你要记住，天才的宿命，就是要一刻不停地解开出现在眼前的难题。
t_en: Fear not, mirror. You have to remember that being a genius means you're destined to solve challenge after challenge. There's no time for rest.

t_ch: …我申请旁边观战，行吗？！
t_en: ...C—Can I just be on the sidelines this time? Pretty please!?

t_ch: 呼，好险…差点碎了一地……
t_en: Phew, that was close... I almost shattered...

t_ch: 别夸大其词，应付这种对手，让我动一个脑细胞都算是高看它了。
t_en: Don't be melodramatic. Working even a single brain cell of mine to take out foes like her is an outright waste of my smarts.

t_ch: 所以，现在三个扇区的麻烦都解决了？这是不是就表示……
t_en: I take it we've taken care of the problems in all three zones? Does that mean...?

t_ch: 在那之前，咱们还得先解决一件小事。
t_en: Not yet. There's something else we need to deal with.

t_ch: 从刚才开始，我们身后好像就一直跟着条小尾巴呢……
t_en: It seems we've had a little tagalong all this while...

t_ch: 您的观察真是细致入微，黑塔女士。需要我们帮忙吗？
t_en: You've got quite the sharp eye, Madam Herta. Would you like us to help?

t_ch: 好啊，把大家都叫出来吧，第四面镜——
t_en: Sure, go ahead and summon the whole team, Fourth Mirror.

t_ch: 我们要做的事如此重要，可不能被一个小小的窃贼给钻了空子啊。
t_en: Considering the gravity of our endeavor, we can't allow a little thief to spoil the show.

t_ch: 「记忆」的寄生虫，你该不会真的以为，这套拙劣的伪装能骗过天才的眼睛吧？
t_en: Hmph, parasite of the Remembrance. Do you truly think your atrocious disguise can outwit the keen sight of a genius like myself?

t_ch: 一旦被我的镜子照到……
t_en: Once you're reflected in my mirrors...

t_ch: …就别想再逃跑咯。
t_en: ...you can forget about escaping.

t_ch: 糟了，这下糟了……
t_en: Oh dear, oh dear. This is bad...

t_ch: 留得青山在，不愁没柴烧…先逃回忆域，以后的事再说！
t_en: Right, stay alive and again I can strive... Retreat into the Memory Zone for now, I can always regroup and try again!

t_ch: 哎，看来有些人没有用心听呢。
t_en: Someone hasn't been paying attention.

t_ch: 我已经说过了吧？就凭你这点本事…也想逃出我的掌心？
t_en: Didn't hear me, did you? With those petty tricks of yours... do you actually think you can slip away right under my nose?

t_ch: 黑塔…黑塔大人！你、你想做什么……
t_en: Herta... Madam Herta, wait! Wh—What do you want with me...?

t_ch: 我…我全都告诉你！忆庭的秘密，他们在觊觎的东西…我全都愿意说出来！请饶、饶了我吧……
t_en: I... I'll spill it all! The Garden's secrets and what they're after... everything! P—Please just spare me...

t_ch: 哎呀，这么紧急的关头，你能想到的救命说辞就只有这么几句？这可不行。
t_en: Well, is that all you've got to say while your life is at stake? That's hardly worth anything.

t_ch: 需要再提醒你一下吗？我是天才俱乐部#83黑塔，解开虚数流溢之谜的学者，虚数坍缩武器的执钥人…你觉得，我需要从你身上获得任何答案吗？
t_en: Need a little refresher? I am Herta, Genius Society #83, the one to unravel the mystery behind the imaginary leakage phenomenon, and keyholder of the Imaginary Implosion Pulse... Do you honestly believe I'd seek any counsel from you?

t_ch: 我…我……
嘘，别出声。这可是我的高光时刻，你只要乖乖听着就行了。
t_en: Hush now, keep quiet. This is my moment to shine, so just listen.

t_ch: 你潜入这里，无非就是为了窃取<color=#dbc291ff>博识尊的记忆</color>，不是么？
t_en: You crept in here to steal <color=#dbc291ff>Nous' memories</color>, didn't you?

t_ch: 把我宝贵的「谒见系统」搞得一团糟，也是为了把我本人引来这里，为你们带路……
t_en: And messing with my precious Audience System is just a ruse to lure me here so I can lead you to THEM...

t_ch: 真可惜，你只差一点就能完成任务，回去交差了……
t_en: What a shame. You're only a step away from completing the mission assigned to you...

t_ch: 唔…唔……
t_en: Mmph...! Nngh...!

t_ch: …开玩笑的，你从头到尾都没有一丝机会，嘻嘻。
t_en: ...As if. Truth is, you never stood a chance from the get-go, teehee!

t_ch: 别看只有第四面镜跟在我身边，它的兄弟姐妹们早就把你的行踪一览无余了。让我想想…该怎么惩罚一个胆大包天的窃贼呢？
t_en: Think I only have Fourth Mirror following me around? Then you've let your guard down, because her siblings have long been watching your every move. Now, let me think... What kind of punishment should I subject a brazen thief like you to?

t_ch: 噢，我有主意了！忆庭的人都很喜欢镜子，对吧？那我干脆就成人之美，让你多跟镜子做做伴吧？
t_en: Ooh, I know! You Garden folk have a fondness for mirrors, huh... How about letting you enjoy some quality time with your favorite object — a mirror?

t_ch: 惩罚的时长…也不该太严苛，就取<color=#dbc291ff>百分之一的人生吧？</color>这点时间，足够你好好反省了。
t_en: As for the length of your punishment... Let's keep it mildly punitive. <color=#dbc291ff>How about a hundredth of a lifetime?</color> That should be enough for some serious self-reflection.

t_ch: 别怕，就算被藏进了镜子里，你还是能为这个世界做出贡献的。你身上那点微薄的命途能量，说不定就能帮我引来机器头的注意呢？
t_en: Aww, cheer up. You can still do your part for this world while trapped in a mirror. For all you know, your weak Path Energy could just help me get Droidhead looking this way.

t_ch: 啊，忘记说了。刚刚提到的<color=#dbc291ff>百分之一</color>……
t_en: Ah, I should make myself clear. When I said <color=#dbc291ff>a hundredth of a lifetime</color>...

t_ch: …乘以的是我的寿命哦。
t_en: ...I meant my lifetime.

t_ch: 哈…一身轻松，总算把问题连根拔起了。
t_en: Ahh, at long last... We can finally be at ease now that we've nipped those problems in the bud.

t_ch: 吧唧吧唧…好吃，好吃……
t_en: Omnomnom... Mmm, this is yummy...

t_ch: 别发出奇怪的动静啊，显得我精神不太正常似的。让那小贼先好好睡一觉吧，晚点再决定该怎么处置她。
t_en: Cut that out! Those weird noises make me seem like I've got a few screws loose. Put that little thief to sleep for now. We can decide her fate later.

t_ch: 哎呀，可爱又可敬的黑塔女士，您的心肠什么时候变得这么软了？
t_en: Oh, sweet and venerable Madam Herta, when did your heart turn this tender?

t_ch: 「谒见系统」已经重启。总算到了这个时候……
t_en: The Audience System has been rebooted. This is finally it...

t_ch: 机器头，我费了这么大功夫只为来到你的面前，可不许无视我啊。
t_en: After all that I've done to reach you, Droidhead... You'd better not snub me.

t_ch: （命途狭间…不是由模拟宇宙构建出来的虚拟情景，而是真实的、可以触及的……）
t_en: (The Path Space... This isn't a figment of the Simulated Universe, but a perfectly tangible and accessible space...)

t_ch: （现在不是感伤的时候。机器头…你会回应我吗？）
t_en: (Right. Now's not the time to wallow in nostalgia. Droidhead... Will you answer me?)

t_ch: 黑塔女士…我们在狭间内捕捉到了一些「声音」。
t_en: Madam Herta... We picked up some voices from the space.

t_ch: 哦？让我听听。
t_en: Hmm? Let me hear them.

t_ch: <unbreak>0001</unbreak>……
t_en: <unbreak>0001</unbreak>...

t_ch: <unbreak>0011</unbreak>……
t_en: <unbreak>0011</unbreak>...

t_ch: 这声音是…二进制运算？难道是机器头？
t_en: Is this... binary arithmetic? Could it be Droidhead?

t_ch: <unbreak>1111</unbreak>……
t_en: <unbreak>1111</unbreak>...

t_ch: <unbreak>0002</unbreak>……
t_en: <unbreak>0002</unbreak>...

t_ch: 不对，二进制运算里怎么会出现「2」？
t_en: Wait, there shouldn't be a "2" in binary arithmetic!

t_ch: 你是在耍我吗……
t_en: Is this some kind of prank...?

t_ch: 黑塔女士！那些声音，又出现了！
t_en: Madam Herta! We're hearing those voices again!

t_ch: 怎么办，我们该怎么办？！
t_en: What now? What should we do!?

t_ch: 你冷静点，第二面镜。它又说了什么，再放给我听听。
t_en: Keep your cool, Second Mirror. What did they say this time? Play it again for me.

t_ch: <unbreak>0126</unbreak>……
t_en: <unbreak>0126</unbreak>...

t_ch: 等等…这道门好像没锁。
t_en: Wait a minute. This door... isn't locked?

t_ch: 门？锁？
t_en: Door...? Locked...?

t_ch: 不对，这根本不是什么命途之声…到底怎么回事？
t_en: No, this isn't the Voice of the Path at all...! What in the world is going on?

t_ch: 咦嘻嘻嘻…黑塔女士，外面的家伙…好像在说些很有趣的话哦？
t_en: Hyuk hyuk hyuk... Madam Herta, those lads outside... seem to be discussing something interesting...

t_ch: 您要不要也来听听？嘻嘻嘻……
t_en: Wanna listen in on them? Hyuk hyuk hyuk...

t_ch: 第三面镜，你能不能收一收那让人起鸡皮疙瘩的笑声？
t_en: Third Mirror! Stop that nightmarish cackle. It's giving me goosebumps.

t_ch: 你刚才提到了「外面的家伙」？难道是……
t_en: Wait, did you say "those lads outside"? Could it be...

t_ch: 欸嘿嘿…女士，要不您自己听？
t_en: Eheheh... Why don't you listen for yourself?

t_ch: 她昏倒了？
t_en: She fainted?

t_ch: 嗯，看来如此。
t_en: Mm-hmm, looks like it.

t_ch: …不对劲，肯定是有人闯进实验室了！
t_en: ...This isn't right. I'm sure we've got intruders in the lab!

t_ch: 要叫醒她么，瓦尔特先生？
t_en: Should we rouse her, Mr. Yang?

t_ch: 嗯，就这么做吧。虽然有些无礼，但我们的确在赶时间。
t_en: ...Let's do it. I'm not sure she'll be happy about it, but we have no time to waste.

t_ch: …等等！等等等等……
t_en: ...Wait! S—Stop whatever you're about to do...

t_ch: 不行，不行，绝对不行！不管你们是谁，别在这个时候打断我！
t_en: No, don't even think about it! Whoever you are, you'd better not interrupt me right now!

t_ch: 我得继续前进…几步，只差几步我就能见到机器头了！
t_en: I must continue forward... Just a few more steps and I'll be able to see Droidhead!

t_ch: 出什么问题了，黑塔女士？
t_en: Is something wrong, Madam Herta?

t_ch: 不，没什么。我只是在想……
t_en: No, it's nothing. I was just thinking...

t_ch: 呃，黑塔女士…从狭间外面传进来的声音，您最好听听……
t_en: Um, Madam Herta... I think you'll want to listen to the voices from beyond this space...

t_ch: 能麻烦你吗，星期日先生？
t_en: I'll leave it to you then, Mr. Sunday.

t_ch: 交给我吧。
t_en: My pleasure.

t_ch: 等等…他们想干嘛？
t_en: Wait... What are they up to?

t_ch: 失礼了，黑塔女士——
t_en: Please pardon me, Madam Herta—

t_ch: 这是…「同谐」？
t_en: Is this... the Harmony?

t_ch: 无理取闹，想靠这种小伎俩影响我？门都没有！
t_en: Ridiculous! Hoping to stop me with such cheap parlor tricks? Not a chance!

t_ch: 她靠着潜意识就轻易化解了「同调」…不愧是令使。
t_en: She easily dispelled the Resonance with her subconscious mind... as expected of an Emanator.

t_ch: 还有别的办法吗？
t_en: Is there something else we can try?

t_ch: 哈哈！不自量力的家伙，还以为自己有能力打断黑塔女士的实验？
t_en: Bahaha! Presumptuous fellows. Who are you to interrupt Madam Herta's experiment?

t_ch: 别理他们，我们接着去见博识尊吧！
t_en: Let's ignore them and keep going. We haven't seen Nous yet!

t_ch: …还是算了，镜子，我们先离开这里吧。
t_en: ...Forget it, mirror. Let's just leave.

t_ch: 出去？您、您的意思是…要在这个时候中断谒见实验？
t_en: Leave? S—Surely you're not suggesting... we stop the Audience Experiment right now?

t_ch: 这太疯狂了，女士！我们费了那么大力气才来到这里，只差一步就能接触到星神本尊了…重启一次「谒见系统」的成本有多高，您也知道！
t_en: That's beyond all reason, ma'am! We went through so much just to get here! Now we're just one step away from making contact with the Aeon THEMSELF... Please, you know how insanely costly it is to reboot the Audience System!

t_ch: 但要是我不管那两个入侵者，继续往前…等机器头向空间站投下目光，那一瞬间的能量保准会把他们烧成焦炭。
t_en: But if I were to ignore those two intruders and continue onward... When Droidhead casts THEIR gaze upon the space station, the blast of energy generated in that instant is sure to burn them to a crisp.

t_ch: 实验还有机会做第二次，人没了可就再没办法复活了。
t_en: Experiments can always be repeated, but humans cannot be brought back to life.

t_ch: 呜呜…博爱又温柔的黑塔女士！本镜可真是跟对了主人啊……
t_en: *sob* Oh Madam Herta, so selfless and kind! I am truly... truly a lucky mirror to have found the best... best possible master...

t_ch: 给我停下，不许用那么恶心的腔调说话！
t_en: Enough. Don't use that nauseatingly fawning tone on me!

t_ch: 哎…看来这次也要半途而废了。
t_en: ...Looks like I'll have to abort this prematurely again.

t_ch: 机器头——我会回来的，你给我等着……
t_en: But mark my words, Droidhead: I will be back for you...

t_ch: …黑塔女士？您醒了？
t_en: ...Madam Herta? You're finally awake.

t_ch: 哎…你们两个……
t_en: ...You two...

t_ch: 我的计划，全毁在你们手上了！
t_en: My plan is ruined! And it's all thanks to you!

t_ch: …您的计划？
t_en: ...Your plan?

t_ch: 很抱歉，黑塔女士…我们并不知道您另有安排。
t_en: My sincerest apologies, Madam Herta... We weren't aware this was a part of your plan.

t_ch: 我们抵达空间站时，只发现这里漆黑一片，我和星期日都以为出现了什么紧急情况。我们一路下行寻找事故根源，没想到会在此处碰到您…的正身。
t_en: The space station was in complete darkness when we arrived, so Sunday and I assumed something bad had happened. We continued further into the space station, hoping to find the cause of the blackout... and that's when we chanced upon your... actual self.

t_ch: 你们…是怎么进来的？
t_en: How did you get in?

t_ch: 那道门没锁。
t_en: The door wasn't locked.

t_ch: 好吧，好吧…怎么说我也是个讲道理的人，这件事是怪不到你们身上。
t_en: Alright, fine... I am a person of reason, after all. You're not to blame for this.

t_ch: 星穹列车的杨先生我有印象，你旁边这位是……
t_en: I am familiar with Mr. Yang of the Astral Express, but the one next to you...

t_ch: …看来黑塔女士对银河间的新闻不太敏感。
t_en: ...Looks like Madam Herta doesn't pay much attention to cosmic news.

t_ch: 请原谅，情急之下我竟忘记了最基本的礼仪——我叫星期日，只是一个普通的搭车客。
t_en: Please pardon my manners. It is unbecoming of me to forget the most basic of courtesies amid all that has happened... My name is Sunday, and I'm just a passenger of the Express.

t_ch: 一个默默无闻的旅人…却是「同谐」的命途行者？哼，谁信啊。
t_en: And this unremarkable passenger... happens to be a Harmony Pathstrider? Hmph, do you take me for a child?

t_ch: 所以，我猜两位大驾光临，肯定不是为了观光吧？
t_en: In any case, I don't suppose you came all the way here just to check on how the space station is doing?

t_ch: 虽然眼下的情境与我们设想中大有出入…但无论如何，空间站没事就好。
t_en: Well, we were worried for a moment there... but it's good to know the space station isn't in any kind of trouble.

t_ch: 黑塔女士，<color=#dbc291ff>我们是为「翁法罗斯」而来。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>We're here because of Amphoreus</color>, Madam Herta.

t_ch: 哦，一点都不意外。
t_en: Hm. Why does that not surprise me?

t_ch: 您想到什么了吗？
t_en: Oh? Something occured to you?

t_ch: 没有，我都和姬子说了，这事怪得很。机器头曾经向那么多世界投去过目光…它们全都刻在我的脑子里，怎么可能有漏网之鱼？
t_en: No, I've told Himeko everything I know. Nothing about the world sounds right. Droidhead has cast THEIR gaze upon numerous worlds in the past... and every one of them is etched in my mind. So how could I possibly not know about it?

t_ch: 要么就是你们被忆庭的人忽悠了。总之，我先用模拟宇宙检索了这四个字，虽然已经筛选掉了一眼无关的结果……
t_en: Perhaps those Garden folks lied to you. Anyhow, I've looked up the name Amphoreus in the Simulated Universe. Though clearly irrelevant results have already been filtered out...

t_ch: 但依旧剩下一份天文数字的清单——什么「『翁法罗斯神王队』下一赛季将使用王者巴维鲁取代队徽」——希望能帮上各位的忙吧。
t_en: There's still an unbelievably long list of generated results. Something about how the Divine Amphoreus team will swap out their current crest emblem for one in King Baviru's likeness in the next season. I hope that can be of some help to you.

t_ch: …无妨，请给我们看看吧。
t_en: ...We may as well take a look at it.

t_ch: 不过我还是好奇，星穹列车不是早就出发了么？那世界真有这么神奇，至于让你们特地折返一趟？
t_en: I still don't get it, though. Didn't the Astral Express already depart some time ago? Is that world so incredibly spectacular that it warrants this return trip?

t_ch: 因为…我们陷入了进退两难的境地。
t_en: Well, that's because... we're caught between a rock and a hard place.

t_ch: 尤其是在亲眼见证后，如果翁法罗斯真的与星神、命途，乃至「智识」密切相关，我们需要天才的智慧。
t_en: If Amphoreus is indeed inextricably entwined with Aeons, Paths, and even the Erudition, we're going to need the wisdom of a genius to help us. Especially now that we've seen the planet for ourselves.

t_ch: 你口中的「进退两难」——要不展开说说吧，杨先生？
t_en: Elaborate a little on this "rock and a hard place" you're caught between?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>对我们而言十分重要的两位同伴，在进入翁法罗斯后便杳无音讯。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>We've lost all contact with two indispensable companions since they entered Amphoreus.</color>

t_ch: 此外…还有另一位同伴，<color=#dbc291ff>身上出现了我们难以解释的可怕症状。</color>
t_en: And that's not all... One of our other companions is <color=#dbc291ff>suffering from a serious and inexplicable affliction.</color>

t_ch: 数个系统时前，星穹列车上……
t_en: Some system hours ago, aboard the Astral Express...

t_ch: 帕姆，你的「员工餐」准备好了吗？
t_en: Pom-Pom, have you prepared our meals already?

t_ch: 当然，已经准备好了帕！三月乘客身体不适，我特地准备了容易消化的列车长定制菜单。
t_en: Of course, the food's ready! March isn't feeling well, so Pom-Pom has specially prepared the conductor's special bland meal that'll be easy on her stomach.

t_ch: 姬子，能麻烦你给她送去吗？
t_en: Can you bring it to her, Himeko?

t_ch: 当然没问题。顺便……
t_en: Gladly. Why don't we also get her a—

t_ch: 不行不行，不可以给病号喝咖啡帕！
t_en: Nuh-uh! No coffee when she's already feeling unwell!

t_ch: 呵…说得也是。
t_en: Heh... I guess you're right.

t_ch: 三月，我进来了哦？
t_en: March? I'm coming in, okay?

t_ch: 咯咯咯…不愧是黑塔女士，这些家伙根本不是对手！
t_en: Brrr... Aaaaand Madam Herta never fails to impress! Those things are no match for you!

t_ch: 哼，小菜一碟~
t_en: Hmph, a piece of cake!

t_ch: 这下清净了！收拾收拾，去下一个扇区吧。
t_en: Now that this place is cleaned up, let's set everything in order and move on to the next zone.

t_ch: 这些家伙…是「记忆」的模因残渣吧？
t_en: These are... memetic remnants of the Remembrance?

t_ch: 就是它们污染、篡改了人偶的设置吗？
t_en: So they were the ones that corrupted and tampered with the settings of my puppets?

t_ch: 虽然已经彻底报废了，不过脑袋倒还完整？
t_en: They're completely battered, save for... their heads?

t_ch: 打人不打脸，对人偶也是一个道理~
t_en: Never strike someone in the face — the same goes for puppets.

t_ch: 更深处好像还有人偶的信号，咱们去看看。
t_en: I'm picking up signals from more puppets somewhere farther in. Let's go take a look.

t_ch: （说起来，印象里小三月从来都没有生过病。）
t_en: (Now that I think about it, I don't think March has ever fallen sick before.)

t_ch: （这次到底是什么原因呢……）
t_en: (What is different about this time, though...?)

t_ch: 使用三月七的相机拍摄吧！
t_en: Let's use March 7th's camera to take photographs!

t_ch: 拍摄远方的巨大球体
t_en: Take a photograph of the giant sphere in the distance

t_ch: 拍摄断崖对岸的大门
t_en: Take a photograph of the gate on the opposite cliff

t_ch: 拍摄悬锋斗技场
t_en: Take a photograph of Kremnos Arena

t_ch: 拍摄十二星宿
t_en: Take a photograph of the twelve constellations

t_ch: 一段时间后……
t_en: Some time later...
Oof...

t_ch: …丹恒？
t_en: ...Dan Heng?

t_ch: 丹恒靠坐在废墟的角落，生死未卜。
t_en: Dan Heng is slumped in the corner of the wreckage, his well-being uncertain.

t_ch: 丹恒，该起床了。
t_en: Dan Heng, time to get up.

t_ch: 落地姿势挺帅，哥们。
t_en: That was one stylish landing, bro.

t_ch: 该给第三代转世起名了…
t_en: Time to come up with a name for the next reincarnation...

t_ch: 等等——你看到丹恒的胸膛在一起一伏，证明他的生命体征尚未薄落。
t_en: Wait— you see Dan Heng's chest rising and falling, proof that life still lingers within him.

t_ch: 用力摇一摇。
t_en: Shake him hard.

t_ch: …纹丝不动，看来你得想点别的法子。
t_en: ...He doesn't even twitch. Looks like you gotta try some new ideas.

t_ch: 人工呼吸？
t_en: Mouth-to-mouth resuscitation?

t_ch: 你想起自己和丹恒、三月七初遇的情景……
t_en: You recall the moment you first met Dan Heng and March 7th...

t_ch: 不，想都别想。
t_en: Nope, ain't no way.

t_ch: 怎么会产生这种想法？你害怕了，恨不得扇一巴掌让自己清醒点！
t_en: How can such a thought even cross your mind? You're scared... You feel like slapping yourself to snap out of it!

t_ch: …不过，也不一定要扇自己？你再次看向丹恒。
t_en: But, come to think of it, you don't necessarily need to slap yourself, right? You take another glance at Dan Heng.

t_ch: …对不住了，丹恒。
t_en: ...Sorry, Dan Heng.

t_ch: 对，就这么做。
t_en: Yep, this is the way.

t_ch: 好像有谁在你耳边大声喊：大胆动手吧！真是恶魔般的靡靡之音。你犹豫再三，终于俯下身……
t_en: It's as if someone is shouting in your ear: "JUST DO IT!" It's veritably the whispers of the devil. After much hesitation, you finally lean down and...

t_ch: …对不住了，丹恒。
t_en: ...Sorry, Dan Heng.

t_ch: 扇他一巴掌！
t_en: Slap him in the face!

t_ch: 你内心冒出了「给他来一下」的冲动，没有理由，没有恩怨，有些想法的涌现就是如此突然。
t_en: You suddenly felt the urge to "slap him" for no reason — There are no grudges. Some thoughts just spontaneously appear like that.

t_ch: …对不住了，丹恒。
t_en: ...Sorry, Dan Heng.

t_ch: …对不住我什么？
t_en: ...Sorry for what?

t_ch: 没什么。
t_en: Oh, it's nothing.

t_ch: 你什么时候醒的？！
t_en: When did you come to!?

t_ch: ——对不住，没能保护好你。
t_en: Uh, pardon me for... not keeping you safe.

t_ch: 唔…我们睡了多久？
t_en: Ugh... How long were we out for?

t_ch: 车厢被击中后，你失去了意识，我把你抬出来后也昏过去了。幸好有车厢保护，否则我们都要粉身碎骨。
t_en: You passed out when our car got hit. I blacked out too, but not before dragging you out. It's a good thing we were in the car, or we would've been crushed to pieces.

t_ch: …先试试能不能联系上列车吧。
t_en: ...We should try making contact with the Express.

t_ch: 果然失败了…这下只能靠你我了。
t_en: I guessed as much... It's no longer working. Looks like it's just the two of us out here now.

t_ch: 得先找个安全的地方当根据地。从四周的建筑，还有这尊雕像来看，翁法罗斯肯定存在文明……
t_en: Let's find someplace safe to set up a base of operations first. This statue and the buildings we've seen so far point to a civilization here on Amphoreus...

t_ch: 坏消息是，他们可能对外来者怀有敌意。
t_en: We can only hope they're not hostile toward outsiders.

t_ch: 这里很危险，不宜久留。
t_en: It's dangerous here. We should leave.

t_ch: 这下又成通缉犯了…
t_en: Are we back to being fugitives again...?

t_ch: 「开拓」就完事了。
t_en: Who cares? We're only here to trailblaze.
Me...

t_ch: 怎么了？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 好像有声音…
t_en: I thought I heard something...

t_ch: 嘘，有动静。
t_en: Shh. Someone's here.

t_ch: 谁在偷看我们？
t_en: Show yourself, peeping Tom!

t_ch: …有人在附近？小心点，拿起武器。
t_en: ...Someone's here? Ready your weapon and stay alert.

t_ch: 先探探门背后是什么。
t_en: Let's find out what's behind that gate.

t_ch: 消失了…完全不给我们摸清状况的机会。你看清那生物的模样了吗？
t_en: It's gone... That happened too fast for us to make any sense of it. Did you see what the creature looked like?

t_ch: 有点像猫。
t_en: A cat of sorts?

t_ch: 有点像兔子。
t_en: A rabbit of sorts?

t_ch: 有点像幕后黑手。
t_en: The mastermind behind everything?

t_ch: 不像是智库中记载的任何一个物种，这也是理所当然，翁法罗斯对于我们是彻彻底底的「未知」。
t_en: It's unlike any of the known species in our data bank. But I guess that's to be expected. We know nothing about Amphoreus, after all.

t_ch: 哪怕是雅利洛-Ⅵ，列车在抵达前也对它的历史和地貌有所了解。但翁法罗斯…黑天鹅口中的几句描述，就是我们对它全部的认知。
t_en: Back when we were trailblazing into Jarilo-VI, we knew its history and terrain even before arriving on the planet. But with Amphoreus... All we have is whatever little Black Swan told us about the world.

t_ch: 但这次「开拓」容不得闪失。
t_en: But we can't afford to screw up this trailblazing expedition.

t_ch: 下一步该怎么做，找当地人？
t_en: What should we do now? Find a local?

t_ch: 恐怕要做好最坏的准备，先不谈语言问题，刚才的袭击…那一击威力相当不俗，很难想象一处与世隔绝的边星竟拥有这种武器技术。
t_en: I'm afraid we must plan for the worst. Putting a potential language barrier aside, that was a powerful attack on us just now... It's hard to imagine a planet as far out and isolated as this having the expertise to create such sophisticated weaponry.

t_ch: 与我手中球棍比何如？
t_en: Is it any match for my baseball bat?

t_ch: 与尔化龙秘法比何如？
t_en: Is it any match for your dragon transmutation?

t_ch: 都说不准，切忌盲目乐观。摸索着前进吧。
t_en: I can't tell for sure. But let's not get our hopes up too high. We can learn and observe as we move.

t_ch: 这附近不像有人居住的样子，刚才的奇妙生物也不见了。不过……
t_en: Well, it looks like no one lives in this area, and that strange creature from before is nowhere to be seen. But...

t_ch: 看，那边。
t_en: Look at that.

t_ch: 那是什么？
t_en: What is that?

t_ch: 好大一个球……
t_en: A giant sphere...

t_ch: 巨构建筑随处可见，当地不仅工程技术发达，还有着某种普世信仰。可惜距离太远，看不清细节。
t_en: Judging from the prevalence of these colossal structures, this civilization has more than just great engineers and architects. The structures also indicate a universal belief system. If only it were closer, I'd be able to make out the details.

t_ch: …要帮拍些照片的话，现在应该正合适。
t_en: ...If you want to take some photos, now would be the perfect time.

t_ch: 远方的巨型球体屹立于天地间，它顶端的天空被撕开…不，更像是它自愿打开了一道圆形的裂口，裂口内的漩涡翻涌倒转，云层周期性地映出金黄色的流光。
t_en: A giant sphere stands between heaven and earth in the distance. At its top, the sky appears torn open — or rather, it seems to have willingly opened a circular rift. Inside, a swirling vortex churns, and the clouds periodically reflect a golden glow.

t_ch: 如果三月七此时在你身旁，她一定用少女的脑回路为眼前的景观找到奇妙且跳脱的譬喻。可惜，现在只能由你来代行团队摄影师的职责了。
t_en: If March 7th were here, she'd definitely use her girlish imagination to come up with some whimsical and creative metaphors for the scenery. Unfortunately, it's up to you to take on the team's photographer duties now.

t_ch: 拍摄远方的巨大球体。
t_en: Photograph the distant giant sphere.

t_ch: 断崖对岸，宏伟的宫殿大门缄口不言，不愿透露一丝藏在身后的历史和秘密。
t_en: Across the cliff, the grand palace gates remain silent, unwilling to reveal any of the history and secrets hidden behind it.

t_ch: 门径之内会是怎样一番景象？你不禁开始放任自己遐想：惨烈的杀戮，喧嚣的盛筵，无底的黑渊……
t_en: What could lie within the gate? You can't help but let your imagination run wild: Bloody carnage, chaotic banquets, bottomless abyss...

t_ch: 然后你回过神来，似乎听到一个调皮的声音在耳边轻语——
t_en: Then you regain focus, hearing a playful voice softly whisper in your ear—

t_ch: 醒~醒，该按快门啦！
t_en: Hellooo~! Snap out of it and click the shutter already!

t_ch: 拍摄断崖对岸的大门。
t_en: Photograph the cliffside gate.

t_ch: （还不错，这几张照片值得摆在照片墙最前边。）
t_en: (These turned out great. Heh, they deserve the most prominent spot on the photo wall.)
...Hmm?

t_ch: 不妙……
t_en: Something's wrong...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，警惕！
t_en: Watch out, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 它们刚才是在这个位置吗？
t_en: Were they at this location before?

t_ch: 似乎来者不善…
t_en: I'm sensing hostility...

t_ch: 这些家伙没有气息…是无机生命？
t_en: These things aren't breathing. ...Inorganic lifeforms?

t_ch: 看来不打算放我们离开，身后是死路和悬崖，只能应战了。
t_en: Looks like they want to keep us here. With a dead end and a cliff edge behind us, there's no other way out. We're going to have to fight them.

t_ch: 所有的「开拓」——
t_en: You can't say it's trailblazing until...

t_ch: ——从挥出第一棒才算正式开始！
t_en: ...you land a hit with your bat!

t_ch: 我们上。
t_en: Let's do this.

t_ch: 别误会，这是为了大家的安全。
t_en: I bear no harm. I just want everyone to be safe.

t_ch: 大家的安全？
t_en: Everyone to be safe?

t_ch: 能听懂他说话哎…
t_en: Hey, I can understand what he's saying...?

t_ch: 大家的安全？
t_en: Everyone to be safe?

t_ch: 在重渊这种地方，手持武器就是一种挑衅。这些士兵都是「纷争」泰坦的爪牙，对外敌向来是赶尽杀绝的。
t_en: Roaming around a place like the Abyss with weapons in tow is a show of provocation. Those soldiers are minions of the Strife Titan. They're not known to stop until all invaders fall under their feet.

t_ch: 如果你们继续挥剑，不但自己会沦为猎物……
t_en: If you continue brandishing your weapons, you won't only be putting yourselves in the crossfire...

t_ch: 还会将它们的悬锋引到无辜者头上。
t_en: Their looming blades will come down on the heads of the innocent.

t_ch: 你大可言语相告，而不是用如此极端的方式。
t_en: A civil approach gets your message across all the same.

t_ch: 两位突然出现，是敌是友犹未可知。况且你们即便手无寸铁，也身怀不容小觑的力量……
t_en: It's difficult to tell if you're friend or foe when you appear out of thin air. Even without your weapons, a great power still lurks within you...

t_ch: 没准身处险境的反而是我呢？可不敢懈怠啊。
t_en: I could be putting myself in harm's way for all I know. I'd be unwise not to take precautions.

t_ch: 那么，能否请你们表明来意，从天而降的客人？
t_en: So, care to share the reason for dropping in on us? Hmm? Visitors from above?

t_ch: 小——白——！你又乱来！跑那么快，还擅自惹事？
t_en: Phai—non—! Look what you did again! Taking off only to get yourself into trouble?

t_ch: 啊！怎么还把人家的武器弄坏了！完了完了，这是和陌生人打交道的礼节吗？
t_en: Agh, you even ruined his weapon! Now you've really gone and done it! This is no way to be treating strangers.

t_ch: 我只是想用最稳妥的方式解决问题。
t_en: I was just trying to fix our problem in the safest way possible.

t_ch: 一点都不稳妥——两位陌生的朋友，请放松放松再放松。大家都是人类，没必要弄得剑拔弩张。
t_en: How is this safe!? Hello, friendly strangers! Let's all simmer down and cool our jets. We're all humans here. No need for things to get so heated.

t_ch: 小白担心你们是天上来的坏人，但*我们*觉得两位没有恶意。啊，得先做个自我介绍——*我们*是雅努萨波利斯的缇宝，这位是…小白，快道歉！
t_en: Snowy's just worried you're baddies from above, but we know you two are nothing of the sort! Oh right, you don't know us yet. We are Tribbie from Janusopolis, and this is... Snowy! Hurry up and apologize to them!

t_ch: 既然缇宝老师都这么说…抱歉，二位降落在危险地带，登场方式又如此特殊，是我警惕心过重了。
t_en: Alright, if you say so, Tribbie... I apologize for my earlier behavior. I was overly wary because both of you landed in this tumultuous region, it put me on high alert.

t_ch: 也可以理解。我们来自天外，是降落在这个世界的「开拓者」。
t_en: I understand. We're Trailblazers from beyond the sky.

t_ch: 就这么招了？
t_en: You're telling them everything already?

t_ch: 他们能理解吗…
t_en: Can they understand that...?

t_ch: 他们都看见了，没必要隐瞒。如果真是袭击我们的人，不会说这么多话。
t_en: There is no point concealing anything. They saw everything. If they wanted us dead, they wouldn't be standing around talking to us.

t_ch: …并非来自「天上」，而是「天外」么。
t_en: ...Not from above the sky but beyond it...?

t_ch: 哇，这下更不得了啦…两位朋友，幸好你们遇见的是*我们*。
t_en: Wow, that's something else altogether... You're lucky you met us, then.

t_ch: 什么意思？
t_en: What do you mean?

t_ch: 意思是我们不会伤害二位，但换作别人就未必了。你们很幸运。
t_en: She means you're lucky because we won't hurt you, but the same can't be said for others.

t_ch: …借一步说话吧，野外实在不安全。你们也看见了，就在神殿里还有不少难民未能脱困。
t_en: ...Let's talk somewhere else. You've seen it first-hand. It isn't safe out here. As you saw, there are still many refugees who haven't been freed in the temple.

t_ch: 我们是来营救并护送他们前往「圣城」奥赫玛的。这也是为什么我要求二位止戈，并反复确认你们并无恶意的原因。如有意外，他们绝无反抗之力。
t_en: We're here to rescue and escort them to the holy city, Okhema. They're the reason I had to be absolutely sure you were not here with ill intent, and why I wanted you to put away your weapons. If something were to occur, they would have no way of protecting themselves.

t_ch: 我是哀丽秘榭的白厄。先前有所冒犯，请接受我的致歉。
t_en: I'm Phainon from Aedes Elysiae. Sorry if I offended you just now.

t_ch: 好奇怪的名字。
t_en: That's an unusual name.

t_ch: 我还是喜欢你桀骜不驯的样子。
t_en: Meh, I preferred it when you were being combative.

t_ch: 明明差一点就能开打了。
t_en: Tsk, a little longer and I would've shown you what I can really do.

t_ch: …你好像很失望的样子。
t_en: ...You sound disappointed.

t_ch: 我叫丹恒。这位是——
t_en: I'm Dan Heng. This is—

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 如此一来，我们也算是不打不相识。给，这是你的武器。
t_en: Friction really does spark friendships, huh? Here, you can have your weapon back.

t_ch: 我是银河球棒侠。
t_en: I'm the Galactic Baseballer.

t_ch: 很响亮的名字，但我猜这是个外号吧？初次见面，是不是报上真名更好？
t_en: That sounds mighty impressive, but I'm guessing it's a nickname? We're meeting each other for the first time, so shouldn't you tell me your actual name?

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 如此一来，我们也算是不打不相识。给，这是你的武器。
t_en: Friction really does spark friendships, huh? Here, you can have your weapon back.

t_ch: 我是失去了球棒的银河球棒侠。
t_en: I'm the Galactic Baseballer who's missing a baseball bat.

t_ch: 球棒？啊，是说这柄神兵利器吗…抱歉，可以用它交换你的名字吗？
t_en: Baseball bat? Oh, you mean this marvel of a weapon...? Sorry, but why don't you tell me your name in return for your weapon?

t_ch: 我是{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 如此一来，我们也算是不打不相识。
t_en: Friction really does spark friendships, huh?

t_ch: 还有你的长枪，放心吧，定性为小白全责，一定让他帮你修好！
t_en: And here's your spear. Don't worry, this is entirely on Snowy. I'll make sure he fixes it for you!

t_ch: 哈哈，这下又得破费请哈托努斯喝几杯了。
t_en: Ahaha... It seems I'll have to treat Chartonus to a couple of drinks again.

t_ch: 我们初来乍到，心中仍有许多疑团。二位若能保障我们的安全，结伴同行自然是更好的选择。
t_en: We've just arrived and still have many questions. If you two can ensure our safety, traveling together would certainly be the best choice.

t_ch: 请带路吧。
t_en: Please lead the way.

t_ch: {M#没人觉得我俩像兄弟吗？}{F#怎么感觉你和我有点像…}
t_en: {M#Does no one else think we look like brothers?}{F#Why do I feel like you and I are kind of alike...}

t_ch: 还真有点？缘分啊——如此一来，我们也算是不打不相识。给，这是你的武器。
t_en: Well how about that? Perhaps it's fate! Friction really does spark friendships, huh? Here, you can have your weapon back.

t_ch: 在想什么吗？
t_en: How do you view the situation?

t_ch: 那个白厄不简单。
t_en: There's more to this Phainon than meets the eye.

t_ch: 同感。能在瞬息间将你缴械，甚至一击斫断这柄长枪，此人绝非等闲之辈。
t_en: I agree. To disarm you in a flash and even snap my spear... He's no ordinary person.

t_ch: 心思也很缜密，对话过程中始终保持着距离。我尝试套取「翁法罗斯」的情报，他俩一唱一和都糊弄过去了。
t_en: He was also quite cautious, keeping his distance throughout the conversation. I tried probing for more information on Amphoreus, but they both casually glossed over it.

t_ch: 但反过来说，这意味着他们没有轻易拿下你我的把握。极端情况下，武力仍是我们的底牌。
t_en: Conversely, I believe this indicates he doesn't believe he can easily subdue us. If things take a turn for the worse, our best bet is still going to be resolution by force.

t_ch: 他们出现的时机太巧了。
t_en: Their appearance was suspiciously well-timed.

t_ch: 这也是一大疑点，那根将车厢摧毁的长矛显然是人造物。
t_en: Yes, the timing is suspect, too. The spear that destroyed our car is definitely man-made.

t_ch: 我怀疑过他们在刻意制造偶遇，但这些难民…怎么看都不像是演的。暂时只能认为，他们和袭击者不是一股势力。
t_en: I suspected they might have staged the encounter, but these refugees... they're clearly not pretending. For now, it's reasonable to assume they're not on the same side as the attackers.

t_ch: 语言居然互通，不奇怪吗？
t_en: How is it even remotely possible that we share a common language?

t_ch: 我也在分析各种可能，但…还没想通。或许这里和雅利洛-Ⅵ一样，在很久以前接入过银轨？可如果是这样，智库里不可能没有记录……
t_en: I've been trying to come up with possible theories for that, though the answer still eludes me. Perhaps like Jarilo-VI, Amphoreus was served by the silver rail eons ago? But the data bank would at least have some record on the planet if it were true...

t_ch: 而且，那女孩一直以「我们」自称，也很奇怪。我不觉得只是某种口癖。
t_en: The way the girl uses "we" and "us" to refer to herself is unusual, too. I doubt it's just a habit of speech.

t_ch: 但在其他疑团面前，语言互通反倒是最不重要的一点了。先上路吧，希望问题能迎刃——咳…水到渠成。
t_en: But compared with our other suspicions, sharing a common language is the least of our concerns right now. As the saying goes, let's get moving like a blade through... You get the point.

t_ch: 你的枪……
t_en: About your spear...

t_ch: 枪断了固然可惜，却换来了有价值的信息：翁法罗斯的锻兵技术能和造出这柄击云的技艺一较高下。
t_en: Though losing the spear is unfortunate, we gained some important insight: Amphoreus' weapon-forging skills are on par with the craftsmanship that forged Cloud-Piercer.

t_ch: 看来这个世界不像我们猜测的那般民智未开，兴许在他们口中的「圣城」，还能有更多意想不到的发现吧。
t_en: Looks like this world is nowhere near as primitive as we had first thought. Perhaps they have more to surprise us with at this "holy city" they mentioned.

t_ch: 没事了，我们走吧。
t_en: Nothing. Let's keep going.

t_ch: 嗯？神殿里似乎有些争吵？进去看看吧，记得表现得友善些。
t_en: Is someone arguing in the temple? Let's take a look, and remember... keep it friendly.

t_ch: 维尔图斯，谁要你通知黄金裔来带我们去什么圣城了？我们是{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的祭司，生来便如无足之鸟，如何能忍受困居一处？
t_en: Virtus! You acted on your own to ask the Chrysos Heirs for help!? To bring us into their so-called holy city, no less! We are priests of {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}, born as footless birds — how could we possibly endure being confined to one place?

t_ch: 诺杜斯先生，有太多同伴死在{RUBY_B#纷争之泰坦}尼卡多利{RUBY_E#}的士兵手里了，我只想大家能安顿下来，睡个好觉。
t_en: We've lost far too many to {RUBY_B#Strife Titan}Nikador{RUBY_E#}'s forces, Mr. Noldus! I only want for everyone to settle down somewhere safe, and to be able to sleep soundly.

t_ch: 失去信仰，失去一切。你真以为奥赫玛容得下我们？末世将近，身在何处都是朝不保夕！
t_en: Lose your faith, and you lose everything. Do you seriously think you can seek shelter in Okhema? Nowhere is safe with the end times looming!

t_ch: 多说无益，要走，你们就走吧，但剩下的人会留在神殿里，接受万径之门，{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的庇佑。
t_en: I've said my piece. Leave, if that's what you want. Everyone else is staying in this temple, where we enjoy the protection of {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus, the Gate of Infinity{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 这位老人家，请你不要冲动——
t_en: Don't make this decision in haste, sir—

t_ch: 黄金裔大人，感谢你们的照拂，但请允许人们为自己选择命运。
t_en: I appreciate the concern, esteemed Chrysos Heir. But please respect that we have the right to choose our own fate.

t_ch: 抱歉，黄金裔大人，让您见笑了。请问这两位是…？
t_en: I'm sorry you had to see that, esteemed Chrysos Heir. Hmm? These two with you are...?

t_ch: 他们也从远方来，是将要前往圣城的旅人。我记得你叫…维尔图斯？叫我白厄就好，刚才发生了什么？
t_en: Travelers from afar. They're also headed for the holy city. If memory serves me right, you are... Virtus? Please, you may call me Phainon. What was that just now?

t_ch: 白厄大人能记得我的名字，不胜荣幸。我们是雅努萨波利斯的祭司，家乡被「纷争」泰坦的军队摧毁，一路辗转流离至此。
t_en: It's an honor you remember me, Phainon. We are priests from Janusopolis. Armies of the Strife Titan destroyed our home, forcing us to flee across many lands until we arrived here.

t_ch: 前些天，我又目睹同伴死在那些怪物手下，实在崩溃了…就向奥赫玛做出祈祷。本以为回应没这么快，时间足够我说服诺杜斯先生，没想到各位来得如此迅速……
t_en: A few days ago, I watched another companion fall to those monsters, and I was shattered... I offered a prayer to Okhema, thinking the answer would take time, at least enough for me to convince Mr. Noldus. I didn't expect your arrival to be so swift...

t_ch: 家家有本难念的经啊。
t_en: Every place has their fair share of troubles.

t_ch: 打的车到了，人还没出门？
t_en: Your ride is here. Why isn't everyone out yet?

t_ch: 不明白你在说什么……
t_en: No idea what you're saying...

t_ch: 那位老人刚才提到了「末世」，还说翁法罗斯哪里都不安全。请问这是……
t_en: The elder spoke of the "end times" just now and claimed that no place in Amphoreus is safe. What did he mean by that...?

t_ch: 这位先生，您竟然不知道吗？这分明是家喻户晓的预言——
t_en: Excuse me, sir? Everyone knows about the prophecy. How is it that you—

t_ch: 维尔图斯，当务之急是把诺杜斯先生劝回来，重渊处处是凶恶的敌人，他们没有自保能力，随时可能身处险境。
t_en: Virtus, right now we must focus on convincing Mr. Noldus to return. The Abyss is swarming with fierce enemies, and they have no way to defend themselves, leaving them vulnerable to all kinds of danger.

t_ch: 两位朋友，我有个不情之请，二位身手不凡，可否劳烦你们走一遭，追回诺杜斯先生？
t_en: My friends, I have a rather bold request. The two of you are quite capable — might I trouble you to fetch Mr. Noldus?

t_ch: 缇宝老师会和二位同行，解答你们的所有疑虑。
t_en: Tribbie will join you. She can also answer any questions you may have.

t_ch: 我们商量一下。
t_en: Please give us a moment to discuss it.

t_ch: 刚才白厄阻止我向难民问话，又说那女孩会解答我们的疑问。意思很明显了。
t_en: Phainon interrupted me as I was trying to gather information from that refugee, and now he's saying the girl will answer our questions. His intent is obvious.

t_ch: 他在向我们隐瞒什么？
t_en: He's hiding something from us?

t_ch: 不是。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 他在向难民隐瞒什么？
t_en: He's hiding something from the refugees?

t_ch: 是的。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: 在他看来，更需要提防的是让其他人发现你我对这个世界一无所知。他想隐瞒「天外来客」的事。
t_en: I believe he thinks it's worse for others to find out we know nothing about this world. He wants to hide the fact that we're visitors from beyond the sky.

t_ch: 目前我们与本地人颇有隔阂，不如先顺着白厄的想法。私下独处时，也探探那位名叫缇宝的女孩态度如何吧。
t_en: There's a rift between us and the locals, so let's play along with Phainon for now. We can also learn what Tribbie thinks when we're alone with her.

t_ch: 我们决定了。既然要与各位同行，理应在力所能及的范围内帮忙。
t_en: Alright, we'll go. If we're to be companions on this journey, it's only right that we offer assistance where possible.

t_ch: 两位朋友真是义薄云天！我会留在此地保护维尔图斯他们，你们速去速回。
t_en: You two are truly honorable! I'll stay behind to guard Virtus and the rest. Go quickly and come back safe.

t_ch: 先斟酌一下？
t_en: Shall we consider our options?

t_ch: 交给我们吧。
t_en: We'll take it from here.

t_ch: 不必担心，我留下来保护附近的人，几位速去速回。
t_en: Don't worry about me. I'll stay behind to protect those in the area. Please make haste.

t_ch: 两位朋友，我猜你们心里有好多疑问，对不对？
t_en: I'm sure you two have a ton of questions, don't you?

t_ch: 为什么要单独聊天，当然是因为越安静的地方越适合交流啦。
t_en: As for why we should speak in private, that's because quiet places are better suited for communicating with one another.

t_ch: 但前面的路…是断的。
t_en: But the road ahead is... cut off.

t_ch: 要拎着我们飞过去吗？
t_en: Are you going to carry and fly us across?

t_ch: 不会要我跳下去吧…
t_en: You're not about to ask me to jump down, right...?

t_ch: 丹恒终于能跳了？
t_en: Dan Heng is finally going to be able to jump?

t_ch: 你在说什么……
t_en: What are you talking about...

t_ch: 嘿，看这反应，你们果然没骗人，真的是初来乍到的旅人啊。那接下来可千万别眨眼——
t_en: Heh. Judging by your reactions, you weren't lying about being newly arrived travelers. In that case, don't blink, or you'll miss the next part.

t_ch: 还有，小白哪有胆子安排*我们*呀，反过来还差不多呢。
t_en: Also, Snowy wouldn't dare make plans for us. It's more like the other way around!

t_ch: 好啦！安静一下，现在*我们*要召唤「神迹」了……
t_en: Alright! Silence, please. We are about to summon a miracle...

t_ch: 「翻越{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的万千门径，我等谦恭之裔矜立于祇前，接受天秤的审判。」
t_en: "We, thy humble descendants, doth surmount the myriad of passages of {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#} to stand before thee and receive the scales of judgment."

t_ch: 「无私的裁决者{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}，请以律法之名，宣我等无罪；称量悬于现世的果实，换取残留于旧日的甜美。」
t_en: "O, selfless arbiter {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}, declare us innocent in the name of the law. Weigh the fruits of the present in exchange for the sweetness of the past."

t_ch: 「我呼唤你，{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}，揭开记忆的帷幕——」
t_en: "We summon thee, {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}, to lift thy curtain of memory..."

t_ch: 呀！怕什么来什么，这下没法悠闲说话了……
t_en: Just what I was afraid of. So much for a peaceful chat...

t_ch: 朋友们，历史课只能一会儿再上了，先帮帮诺杜斯先生！
t_en: The history lesson will have to wait, my friends. We must help Mr. Noldus!

t_ch: 呼…感谢三位的救命之恩。
t_en: Whew... Thank you. The three of you saved our lives.

t_ch: 诺杜斯先生，维尔图斯说的没错呀，你们在这儿可危险了，还是跟*我们*一起回奥赫玛吧。
t_en: Mr. Noldus, Virtus was right. It's too dangerous for all of you to be here. Please come back to Okhema with us.

t_ch: 孩子，我想我表达得很清楚了，祭司们宁可死在追逐信仰的道路上，也不愿在异邦他乡苟活。
t_en: I believe I've made my stance clear, my child. Us priests would rather die in the pursuit of faith than live in a foreign land.

t_ch: 我看你还挺想活的…
t_en: It looks to me like you still prefer living...

t_ch: 老头，别把自己骗了…
t_en: Don't lie to yourself, old man...

t_ch: 先生，*我们*无比理解你对泰坦的信仰。也许在你看来，庇佑圣城的{RUBY_B#<color=#dbc291ff>负世之泰坦</color>}<color=#dbc291ff>刻法勒</color>{RUBY_E#}是异邦的神明。
t_en: Sir, we deeply understand your faith in the Titans. Perhaps in your eyes, {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, the protector of the holy city, is but a god from a foreign land.

t_ch: 但*我们*以万径之门，{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的名义向你起誓，只要黄金裔一日尚存，奥赫玛就会保护每一位泰坦的子民，*我们*不分彼此。
t_en: But we swear to you in the name of {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus,{RUBY_E#} the Gate of Infinity that so long as the Chrysos Heirs live, Okhema will protect all followers of the Titans. We are one and the same.

t_ch: 以{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}的名义起誓？你是谁……
t_en: You swear in the name of {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}? Who are you...

t_ch: …不记得了吗，诺杜斯？
t_en: Don't you remember, Noldus?

t_ch: *我*的名字，是缇里西庇俄丝。
t_en: Our name is Tribios.

t_ch: 什么，竟然是大祭司大人？！
t_en: What? You're the high priest!?

t_ch: 您怎么变成了这幅孩童模样？不对，我一介部族祭司，竟在您面前妄言信仰，真是不识好歹！请您恕罪……
t_en: Why do you look like a child? No, to think that I, a mere tribal priest, dared lecture you on faith. How ludicrous! Please forgive my transgressions...

t_ch: 诺杜斯，*我们*不愿强迫你。但你也要为部族的同胞们多考虑。
t_en: Noldus, we do not wish to force you, but please think of the people in your tribe.
